name: contrastive_repr_v1
description: Contrastive/metric representation learning for Leduc state embeddings. Tests whether multi-dimensional embeddings trained via contrastive loss capture structure beyond scalar V(s).

results.key_finding: Contrastive losses produce genuinely multi-dimensional representations (4-5 eff dims) where TD(0) collapses to 1D. L2 (Rank-N-Contrast) is most balanced: best dimensionality spread + state identity preservation. No shortcut exploitation detected (D8). No distributional structure found — training signal doesn't supervise variance.
